span.p: Does the run involve DB writes - deploy triggers - secret rotation - or force-push?

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p: HIGH BLAST - frontier tier mandatory - human gate required before run and at each irreversible step

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p: Does the run open a PR or modify the test suite?

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p: Is the scope envelope explicitly defined - files allowed and excluded?

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p: Define scope envelope first - return to this tree after scoping

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p: MEDIUM BLAST - balanced default or raised reasoning - PR review is the gate

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p: Is the context demand High or Very High?

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p: Check context-window-planning skill - chunk if feasible before upgrading tier

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p: LOW BLAST - balanced default tier - no gate required
